title: senecio doronicum subsp. transylvanicus
intro: Senecio doronicum subsp. transylvanicus, also known as Transylvanian tansy, is a subspecies of the perennial plant Senecio doronicum, belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is said to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Senecio doronicum subsp. transylvanicus can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
